name: Campbell
description_short: Campbell Federal Credit Union is an NCUA-insured, member-owned credit union offering savings, checking, loans, and digital banking services to members in New Jersey.
description_long:
Campbell Federal Credit Union is a federally insured credit union operating as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution. Based in Maple Shade, New Jersey, the credit union has established itself as a community-focused financial services provider with a physical branch presence and digital accessibility for members.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including savings and checking accounts, IRAs, youth accounts, auto and recreational loans, personal loans, home equity lines of credit, mortgages, MasterCard credit cards, and student loans. Their service offerings extend to mobile banking, online banking, bill pay, Visa debit and ATM cards, Bank by Phone (telephone banking at 1-800-544-0949), money transfers, shared branching access, and Zelle money transfer functionality. Members can access their accounts through multiple channels: in-person at physical branches, online, via mobile app, or through telephone banking.

Campbell distinguishes itself through its commitment to member convenience and accessibility. The credit union provides shared branching services allowing members to conduct transactions at other credit union branches, comprehensive fraud alert measures (including warnings about impersonation scams), and transparent communication through newsletters and updates. They maintain a routing number (#236077658) for direct deposits and transfers, and offer member discounts and financial education resources. The institution emphasizes security by explicitly stating they will never request sensitive information like account numbers, PINs, or card details over the phone.

As a credit union, Campbell Federal operates under NCUA federal insurance protection rather than FDIC insurance, making it suitable for members seeking traditional banking services with member-ownership benefits. However, the website provides limited detail regarding specific interest rates, loan terms, fee structures, or membership eligibility criteria. The organization appears well-established with historical information available, though detailed competitive analysis of rates and terms would require additional research.
services: Visa debit cards and ATM access
Shared branching at other credit unions
pros: NCUA federally insured member deposits, providing safety comparable to FDIC banking
Multiple account access channels: in-person branches, mobile app, online banking, telephone banking, and shared branching at other credit unions
Comprehensive loan product suite including auto, personal, home equity, mortgages, and student loans in one institution
Zelle integration for rapid digital money transfers between members and external accounts
Proactive fraud prevention with clear communication about scams and member protection policies
Youth accounts available, supporting financial education for younger members
Member discounts and financial education resources provided to support financial wellness
cons: Limited geographic accessibility with primary office in Maple Shade, New Jersey; unclear if membership is geographically restricted
Website lacks specific interest rates, APRs, and fee schedules in readily accessible sections, requiring members to contact the institution for pricing
No information provided about minimum deposit requirements, membership eligibility criteria, or account opening processes on the main website
Mobile banking and online banking mentioned but detailed feature comparisons and functionality not described on website
Historical focus on New Jersey area may limit appeal and accessibility for out-of-state consumers
best_for: New Jersey residents seeking comprehensive banking services through a member-owned, not-for-profit institution
Borrowers needing multiple loan products (auto, personal, home, mortgage) from a single trusted lender
Members who value multi-channel access and prefer flexibility between digital banking and in-person branch services
Consumers prioritizing fraud protection and transparent communication about security threats
diagnosis: Campbell Federal Credit Union is best for New Jersey residents and members seeking traditional comprehensive banking services with the member-ownership benefits of a credit union and NCUA federal insurance protection. The primary limitation is the lack of transparent rate and fee information on their website, requiring prospective members to contact the institution directly to compare offerings with competing financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Campbell — Credit Union in Maple Shade, NJ | CreditDoc
meta_description: Campbell — NCUA-insured credit union in Maple Shade, NJ. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
